agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority 2026-2030 Strategic Plan up for council approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's (BHA) 2026-2030 Strategic Plan. This plan outlines the BHA's goals and priorities for building and managing affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years. If approved, it will guide how the BHA operates and what housing projects it pursues. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the plan at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this plan could affect future housing development and rental opportunities in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BHA - 2026-2030 STRATEGIC PLAN
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2026-2030 Strategic Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to pay 2026 UBCM membership dues
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the payment of membership dues to the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) for 2026. UBCM is an organization that represents local governments across BC and advocates for their interests. This is a routine annual expense that keeps Burnaby a member of the group.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
UBCM 2026 MEMBERSHIP DUES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for payment of the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) 2026 membership dues.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for water metering technology
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of water metering technology. If approved, this would allow the city to buy new water meters and related equipment. This is a proposed decision — it has not been finalized yet. The purpose is to upgrade the city's water metering system, which could eventually affect how water use is measured and billed for businesses and residents.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY WATER METERING TECHNOLOGY
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Supply and Delivery of Water Metering Technology.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on modular housing four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a contract award for a pilot project to build a modular four-plex housing unit at 8315 14th Avenue. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the contract would allow the city to move forward with constructing a four-unit modular home as a test project. The goal is to see if modular housing can be a faster, cheaper way to add affordable homes in Burnaby. The council has not made a final decision yet; this item is up for approval at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX UNIT AT 8315 14TH AVENUE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on 19 contracts for hired equipment services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding 19 separate contracts for hired equipment services. These contracts would allow the city to rent or lease equipment like trucks, excavators, and other machinery for city projects. If approved, the contracts would be awarded to various companies. This is a proposed decision — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - HIRED EQUIPMENT SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval to award nineteen separate contracts (CA) for Hired Equipment Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on asphalt paving contract renewal for Engineering
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on renewing a contract for asphalt paving services used by the Engineering Department. This is a proposed contract renewal — it has not been approved yet. If approved, the city would continue using the same paving company for road and infrastructure work. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CR - ASPHALT PAVING SERVICES - ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Asphalt Paving Services – Engineering Department.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: High-density mixed-use development at 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a proposal for an amended high-density mixed-use development at 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court. The project would combine commercial and residential space, including both strata (individually owned units) and rental units. Council will vote on whether to authorize staff to work with the developer on a detailed plan. A final decision on the project itself will come later, after a more detailed report is presented.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the upcoming council decision and public hearing dates — this could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
PDC - AMENDED DEVELOPMENT PROPOSAL ENQUIRY FOR 3856 AND 3870 CARRIGAN COURT - PROPOSED AMENDED HIGH-DENSITY MIXED-USE COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL STRATA AND RENTAL DEVELOPMENT
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to work with the applicant towards the preparation of a suitable plan of development, in accordance with Section 3.0 of this report, on the understanding that a more detailed report will be submitted for Council consideration at a later date.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mixed-use development at 3900 Grand Promenade, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a rezoning application (REZ #24-23) for properties at 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place. A proposed bylaw would change the zoning to allow a mixed-use commercial and residential development. This is a first step — Council will vote on whether to send the proposal to a public hearing. If approved, the project could bring new shops and homes to the area.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to have a say on the proposed development.

key_dates.label: Council vote on first reading of rezoning bylaw (REZ #24-23)

agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
PDC - REZ #24-23 - 3900 GRAND PROMENADE, 9850 AUSTIN RD, AND 9858 AND 9898 GATINEAU PLACE - AMENDED MIXED-USE COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #24-23 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Twelve-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to move a rezoning application forward for a proposed twelve-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. If approved at a future meeting, the rezoning would allow a new development with both commercial and residential space. This is a first step — the proposal has not been approved yet, and Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to have a say on the proposed rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - REZ #25-09 - 6535 KINGSWAY - PROPOSED TWELVE-STOREY MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-09 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to move a rezoning application forward for a five-storey self-storage facility at 2757 Norland Avenue. The proposal, called REZ #25-21, would change the land-use rules for that property to allow the new building. Council will first consider sending the application to 'First and Second Reading' — which are early steps in the approval process — at a future meeting. This is not a final decision yet; it is a vote on whether to keep the process moving.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the upcoming council meeting date — if you own or operate a business nearby, consider contacting your councillor or attending the meeting to share your views.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PDC - REZ #25-21 - 2757 NORLAND AVENUE - FIVE-STOREY SELF-STORAGE FACILITY
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-21 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy up for Council endorsement
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing a new Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy. This proposed strategy outlines a plan to reduce poverty in the city over the long term. If endorsed, it would guide future city policies and programs. The strategy is not yet in effect — Council will decide whether to give it the green light at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if endorsed, this strategy could shape future city programs and policies that affect your business and employees.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
SPC - BURNABY POVERTY REDUCTION STRATEGY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity reports for Jan–Feb 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider two building permit activity reports covering January and February 2026. These reports summarize construction activity in Burnaby based on permits issued during those months. The purpose is to inform Council about recent development trends. No new rules or fees are proposed — this is an information item only.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 1 FROM JANUARY 1 - JANUARY 31, 2026, AND BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 2 FROM FEBRUARY 1 - FEBRUARY 28, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review 2026 city investment forecast
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to review a year-end report on the city's 2025 investment program and a forecast for 2026. This report updates Council on how the city's money was invested last year and what the plan is for next year. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider the report and forecast at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the 2026 forecast will guide how the city invests public funds in the coming year.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - CITY INVESTMENTS - 2025 YEAR END REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the 2025 Investment Program and present a forecast for 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive status update on significant civic projects for March 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive a status update on significant civic projects at its March 2026 meeting. This report will provide an overview of how major city-funded construction and development projects are progressing. While no new rules or fees are being proposed, the update may signal upcoming changes to project timelines, budgets, or scope that could affect local businesses, especially those in construction and development. The decision is to receive the report for information only — no immediate action is required from the public.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MARCH 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2050 plan implementation — council to vote on next steps
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a plan that lays out the next steps for putting the 'Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan' into action. This 'Implementation Action Plan' would guide how the city grows over the next 25 years, including where new housing, businesses, parks, and transportation go. A proposed bylaw or policy package could change rules for development, zoning, and business permits. Council will review the overview at an upcoming meeting before deciding whether to move forward. Nothing is final yet — this is a proposal for discussion and a potential vote.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on how the plan affects your business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
PDC - OCP: BURNABY 2050 IMPLEMENTATION ACTION PLAN PROJECT INITIATION
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey mixed-use tower at 5058 Imperial Street, Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning that would allow a 20-storey mixed-use building at 5058 Imperial Street. The plan includes market rental apartments and a double-height commercial space at street level. This proposal replaces a previously approved plan for a six-storey building with both market and non-market (affordable) rental units on the same site. The new proposal is larger and does not include the non-market housing that was part of the earlier approval. Council will consider giving first and second reading to the rezoning bylaw, which is a step toward a final decision. A public hearing may be scheduled before the bylaw is adopted.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby Council to voice your support or concerns about this rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14802 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026 - REZ #25-13 (5058 Imperial Street)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 20-storey mixed-use multiple-family market rental residential development fronting Imperial Street with a double-height commercial storey at street level. The proposed development concept supersedes a previously approved plan for a six-storey market and non-market rental apartment building on the site approved through REZ #20-36

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Master plan amendment for 6800 Lougheed Highway in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would update the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway. The amendment would change the approved site plan to allow for taller buildings and different building shapes, in line with the city's new Official Community Plan (OCP 2050) and new height-based zoning rules (called R6 and CM1). It would also add more open space on the site. No actual construction or subdivision is being proposed at this time — this is just a change to the long-term plan for the property.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed master plan changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14803 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026 - REZ #25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy)
Purpose: to advance a Master Plan (MP) amendment to the Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan to establish a revised site plan with updated height and massing in alignment with the recently adopted OCP 2050 and associated height-based zoning districts (R6 and CM1), as well as expanded open space provisions for the site. No development or subdivision is proposed through the subject MP amendment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 49-storey tower with retail at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning that would allow a 49-storey apartment tower at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The building would include a 9-storey terraced base (called a podium) and street-level retail shops. This is only the first and second reading of the bylaw — it has not been approved yet. A public hearing and final vote are still needed before anything is decided.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak for or against the proposal.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14804 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026 - REZ #25-20 (2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a single high-rise apartment tower of up to 49 storeys inclusive of a 9 storey terraced podium with street fronting retail

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby parking meter bylaw changes for EV charging fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to the Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update the rules and fees for the city's public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. If approved, it could change how much you pay to charge your EV at public meters in Burnaby. The decision has not been made yet — this is a final adoption vote on a proposal that has already been introduced.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, the new EV charging rates will apply to public meters in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019 (public EV network)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby EV network fee changes up for final council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would amend the city's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for the public electric vehicle (EV) network. If approved, this would update the fees and charges for using Burnaby's public EV charging stations. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the amendment at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, EV charging fees could change. No action needed now.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (public EV network)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to enforce public EV network rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This change would allow the city to enforce rules related to the public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. If approved, the city could issue tickets or fines for things like parking violations or misuse at public EV charging stations. The amendment is up for final adoption at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect how you use or manage public EV charging stations in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (public EV network)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed rezoning application (REZ #25-13) for 5058 Imperial Street. A petition from nearby residents, including those at 5051 Imperial Street, has been submitted opposing this rezoning. The petition asks Council to reject the application. This is a proposed change to the property's zoning rules — zoning controls what can be built or operated on a piece of land. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit your written comments to Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
12.1
PETITION: Opposition to REZ #25-13 - 5058 Imperial Street
Purpose: To provide a petition from the residents of 5051 Imperial Street and surrounding neighbours in opposition to REZ #25-13 – 5058 Imperial Street.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby Housing Authority's 2026-2030 Strategic Plan
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation's (BHA) 2026-2030 Strategic Plan. This plan sets out the BHA's goals and priorities for the next five years to guide its work on housing in Burnaby. The decision was made at the March 24, 2026 council meeting, and the plan is now in effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BHA - 2026-2030 STRATEGIC PLAN
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report seeking
Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2026-
2030 Strategic Plan.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 144
THAT the 2026-2030 Strategic Plan of the Burnaby Housing Authority
Corporation, substantially in the form attached as Attachment 1 to the report
titled "BHA – 2026-2030 Strategic Plan”, dated March 24, 2026, and as described
in section 3.1 of the report, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $36,250.57 for 2026 UBCM membership dues
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved payment of $36,250.57 for the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) 2026 membership dues. This is an annual fee that allows Burnaby to be a member of UBCM, an organization that represents local governments across the province and advocates for cities like Burnaby on issues such as housing, infrastructure, and public safety.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.2
UBCM 2026 MEMBERSHIP DUES
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report seeking Council
approval for payment of the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM)
2026 membership dues.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 145
THAT payment of the invoice from the Union of BC Municipalities for the 2026
membership dues in the amount of $36,250.57, including taxes, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $4.2M contract for new water metering technology
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a $4,200,000 contract award to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. for the supply and delivery of water metering technology. The contract was approved at the March 24, 2026 council meeting, with final payment based on actual quantities delivered and unit prices as proposed. Three councillors opposed the decision.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.3
CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY WATER METERING TECHNOLOGY
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Supply and Delivery of Water
Metering Technology.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 146
THAT a contract award to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. for an
estimated total cost of $4,200,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled
“CA – Supply and Delivery of Water Metering Technology” dated March 24, 2026
be approved; and
THAT final payment be based on the actual quantities of services delivered and
unit prices as proposed.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Councillors Calendino, Dhaliwal and Lee)

minutes_briefs.title: Council referred modular housing four-plex at 8315 14th Ave back to staff
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to refer a proposed modular housing four-plex project at 8315 14th Avenue back to city staff for further review. The project was a pilot to build a four-plex using modular construction. Council decided the report needed more work to make sure the project is managed efficiently before it can move forward. No contract was awarded, and the project is on hold pending staff's additional review.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX UNIT AT 8315
14TH AVENUE
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council
approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the development of a modular four-plex
housing unit as a pilot project.
Arising from discussion, the original recommendation was not voted on, and
Councillor Gu introduced the following motion:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 147
THAT the report titled “CA – Development of Modular housing Four-Plex Unit at
8315 14th Avenue”, dated March 24, 2026, be now REFERRED back to staff for
further review to ensure project management efficiencies.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 19 contracts for hired equipment services
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved awarding 19 separate contracts for hired equipment services. The contracts cover various types of equipment the city needs to rent for projects. Final payment will be based on the actual amount of services used, at the unit prices submitted by each contractor.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - HIRED EQUIPMENT SERVICES
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council
approval to award nineteen separate contracts (CA) for Hired Equipment
Services.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 148
THAT a contract award to 19 separate contractors, as outlined in the report titled
“CA - Hired Equipment Services” dated March 24, 2026, be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantity of services delivered and
unit prices as submitted.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved $1.16M contract renewal with Blackrete Paving Ltd.
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved a contract renewal with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for asphalt paving services. The contract is valued at an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes. Final payment will be based on the actual amount of work done and the agreed-upon unit prices, not a fixed total. This decision was made at the March 24, 2026 council meeting.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CR - ASPHALT PAVING SERVICES - ENGINEERING DEPARTMENT
The Deputy General Manager Finance submitted a report seeking Council
approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for Asphalt Paving Services – Engineering
Department.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 149
THAT a contract renewal to Blackrete Paving Ltd., for an estimated cost of
$1,155,000 including taxes, as outlined in the report titled “CR – Asphalt Paving
Services – Engineering Department” dated March 24, 2026 be approved; and
THAT final payment will be based on actual quantities of services delivered and
contracted unit prices.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning application for high-density development on Carrigan Court
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to accept a rezoning application for 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court. The project is an amended high-density mixed-use development that will include both commercial and residential space, with some units for sale (strata) and some for rent. Council authorized staff to keep working with the developer to prepare a detailed plan. A more complete report will come back to Council for a final decision later. Councillor Dhaliwal voted against the motion.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Watch for the next public report on this development if your business is in the area.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.1
PDC - AMENDED DEVELOPMENT PROPOSAL ENQUIRY FOR 3856 AND
3870 CARRIGAN COURT - PROPOSED AMENDED HIGH-DENSITY MIXED-
USE COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL STRATA AND RENTAL DEVELOPMENT
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking
Council authorization to work with the applicant towards the preparation of a
suitable plan of development, in accordance with Section 3.0 of this report, on
the understanding that a more detailed report will be submitted for Council
consideration at a later date.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 150
THAT staff be authorized to accept a rezoning application and to continue to
work with the applicant towards the preparation of a suitable plan of development
for advancement to Council, in accordance with Section 3.0 of the report titled
“Amended Development Proposal Enquiry For 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court –
Proposed Amended High Density Mixed-Use Commercial/Residential Strata and
Rental Development”, dated March 4, 2026, of the Planning and Development
Committee meeting, for the development of an amended high density mixed-use
commercial/residential strata and rental development, on the understanding that
a more detailed report will be submitted for Council consideration at a later date.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Councillor Dhaliwal)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 3900 Grand Promenade mixed-use development
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted to advance a rezoning application (REZ #24-23) for 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place. The approved changes replace some commercial office space with hotel and restaurant uses, add 362 new market strata residential units, and increase the height of Tower 1B by seven storeys and Tower 1A by four storeys. The developer will also provide the inclusionary requirement (affordable housing contribution) for Phase 1 only, which will be transferred to the Carrigan site (3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court). The previous rezoning bylaw (Bylaw No. 14737) will be abandoned once the new bylaw gets Second Reading. A new Housing Agreement for the Carrigan site was also authorized.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own or operate a business near this site, review the updated development plans to understand how the new hotel, restaurant, and additional residential units may affect traffic, parking, and local demand.
minutes_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.2
PDC - REZ #24-23 - 3900 GRAND PROMENADE, 9850 AUSTIN RD, AND 9858
AND 9898 GATINEAU PLACE - AMENDED MIXED-USE
COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking
Council authorization to forward REZ #24-23 to First and Second Reading at a
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment
is to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32
(Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and
included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower
1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part
of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to
replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses,
and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept
approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower
1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, the applicant is proposing to
provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be
transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous
rezoning approval.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 151
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #24-23 be prepared and advanced to First and
Second Reading at a future Council meeting;
THAT the predecessor Rezoning Bylaw, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965,
Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 (Bylaw No. 14737), be abandoned contingent
upon the granting by Council of Second Reading of the subject Rezoning Bylaw;
THAT a replacement Housing Agreement be authorized for the Carrigan site
(3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court – REZ #20-18) according to the amended terms
outlined in Section 3.2 of the report titled “REZ #24-23 – 3900 Grand
Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place – Amended
Mixed-Use Commercial/Residential Development” dated March 4, 2026, of the
Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be
authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report 6 be established as
prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ
#24-23.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Mayor Hurley, Councillor Dhaliwal)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw for 6535 Kingsway. The decision allows a 12-storey mixed-use building with 290 residential units, ground-floor commercial space facing Kingsway, and underground parking. The project must meet conditions listed in the report before final approval. This rezoning is consistent with the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - REZ #25-09 - 6535 KINGSWAY - PROPOSED TWELVE-STOREY
MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking
Council authorization to forward REZ #25-09 to First and Second Reading at a
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment
is to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family
development with a double-height commercial storey at street level, comprised of
290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and
underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the
Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 152
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-09 be prepared and advanced to First and
Second Reading at a future Council meeting;
THAT a Housing Agreement, if required, be authorized according to the terms
outlined in Section 3.4 of the report titled “REZ #25-09 – 6535 Kingsway –
Proposed Twelve-Storey Mixed-Use Development” dated March 04, 2026, of the
Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be
authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as
prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ
#25-09.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for five-storey self-storage at 2757 Norland Avenue
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted to advance a rezoning bylaw that will allow a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. The bylaw was sent to first and second reading, and no public hearing will be held because the project is consistent with the city's Official Community Plan. Council also set conditions that must be met before the bylaw can be finally adopted.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PDC - REZ #25-21 - 2757 NORLAND AVENUE - FIVE-STOREY SELF-
STORAGE FACILITY
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking
Council authorization to forward REZ #25-21 to First and Second Reading at a
future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment
is to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The
proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies
and approved transitionary provisions.  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 153
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-21 be prepared and advanced to First and
Second Reading at a future Council meeting;
THAT a Public Hearing not be held for the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-21, as it
is consistent with the Burnaby Official Community Plan; and
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report titled “REZ #25-21 – 2757
Norland Avenue- Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility”, dated March 4, 2026, of the
Planning and Development Committee meeting, be established as prerequisites
to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-21.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Councillor Gu)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Burnaby's Poverty Reduction Strategy
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has unanimously endorsed the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy. This means the city has officially adopted a plan to reduce poverty locally. Council also authorized staff to work with community partners to create an implementation plan. In addition, the Mayor will write to the provincial government asking for renewed funding to help communities carry out local poverty reduction projects.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is required for your business. Stay informed as the implementation plan develops.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.5
SPC - BURNABY POVERTY REDUCTION STRATEGY
The Social Planning Committee (SPC) submitted a report seeking Council
endorsement of the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 154
THAT the Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy, provided as Attachment 1 to the
report titled “Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy,” dated March 11, 2026, of the
Social Planning Committee meeting, be endorsed;
THAT staff be authorized to work with community partners to undertake the
development of an implementation plan; and
THAT the Mayor write a letter to the Minister of Social Development and Poverty
Reduction advocating to re-establish an ongoing funding program in partnership
with the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) to support communities in
implementing local poverty reduction initiatives and forward a copy of this report
and attachments.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council gave first and second reading to rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to give first and second reading to Bylaw 14802, which would rezone 5058 Imperial Street. This is an early step in the approval process; the bylaw must still pass third reading and be adopted before it takes effect. The vote was carried, with Mayor Hurley and Councillors Keithley, Lee, and Wang opposed.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Monitor for the public hearing date and third reading if your property or business is affected by this rezoning.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14802 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026 -
REZ #25-13 (5058 Imperial Street)
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 160
THAT Bylaw No. 14802 be now read a first and second time.
CARRIED
(Opposed: Mayor Hurley, Councillors Keithley, Lee, and Wang)

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved first and second reading of rezoning bylaw for 6800 Lougheed Hwy
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to give first and second reading to Bylaw 14803, which would rezone 6800 Lougheed Highway. This is an early step in the rezoning process — the bylaw still needs a public hearing and third reading before it can take effect. The rezoning would change what types of buildings and uses are allowed on this property.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Monitor for the public hearing date if you own or operate a business at or near 6800 Lougheed Hwy — this is your chance to provide input before the bylaw is finalized.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2 #14803 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 5, 2026 -
REZ #25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy)
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 159-1
THAT Bylaw No. 14803 be now read a first and second time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved first and second reading of rezoning bylaw for Springer Avenue properties
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to give first and second reading to Bylaw 14804, which would rezone 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. This is an early step in the rezoning process — the bylaw still needs a public hearing and third reading before it can take effect. The rezoning would amend the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to allow a different use or density on these two properties.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Monitor for the public hearing date on this rezoning — property owners and businesses in the area may want to attend or submit comments.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3 #14804 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026 -
REZ #25-20 (2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue)
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 159-2
THAT Bylaw No. 14804 be now read a first and second time.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved final adoption of Burnaby parking meter and EV charging bylaw
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to give final approval to Bylaw 14805, which updates the city's rules for parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters. This bylaw amends the existing 2019 Parking Meter and EV Charging Meter Bylaw. The decision is now complete and the updated rules are in effect.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates or uses parking meters or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw for any changes to rates or rules.
minutes_briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1 #14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter
Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Resolution No. 2026 - 161-1
THAT Bylaw No. 14805 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby's updated fees and charges bylaw
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved Bylaw 14806, which amends the city's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This means the city has officially updated its list of fees for various permits, licences, and services. The new fee amounts are now in effect. Business owners should check the updated bylaw to see if any fees that apply to their operations have changed.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the new Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw to see if any fees for your business permits or licences have changed.
minutes_briefs.industries: Liquor, Cannabis, Construction & Development, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2 #14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment
Bylaw No. 2, 2026
Resolution No. 2026 - 161-2
THAT Bylaw No. 14806 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council adopted Burnaby's updated bylaw notice enforcement rules
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved Bylaw No. 14807, which amends the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This means the city has updated the rules for how bylaw notices (tickets or fines for things like parking, property, or noise violations) are enforced. The change is now law and in effect.
minutes_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3 #14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment
Bylaw No. 2, 2026
Resolution No. 2026 - 161-3
THAT Bylaw No. 14807 be now read a final time and adopted.  
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council received petition opposing rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to receive a petition from residents opposing the rezoning application REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street. The petition, which now has 190 signatures from Burnaby residents, was received for information only. Council did not approve or reject the rezoning itself — this was simply a procedural step to formally accept the petition into the public record. The rezoning application (Bylaw No. 14802) remains under consideration.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
12.1 PETITION: Opposition to REZ #25-13 - 5058 Imperial Street
The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a petition from the residents
of 5051 Imperial Street and surrounding neighbours in opposition to REZ #25-13
- 5058 Imperial Street (Bylaw No. 14802).
Note from Legislative Services: A supplementary letter and a petition,
containing 15 signatures of Burnaby residents, was received on March 24, 2026,
regarding the REZ #25-13, which brings a total of signatures to 190.

MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 162
THAT the attached Petition from the residents of 5051 Imperial Street and
surrounding neighbours regarding the opposition to REZ #25-13 – 5058 Imperial
Street, received by Council on March 24, 2026, be received for information.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Joanne Morgan as Burnaby's 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously approved Joanne Morgan as the 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the Year. The Kushiro Cup presentation will take place at the 2026 City Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026. Council also authorized staff to contact the recipients and nominators before the public meeting, and released the related report.

key_dates.label: Kushiro Cup presentation at City Appreciation Dinner

minutes_briefs.source_body:
14.1 Resolution No. C2026 - 029
THAT Joanne Morgan be approved as Burnaby’s 2025 Outstanding Citizen of the
Year, and that the Kushiro Cup presentation take place at the 2026 City
Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026;
THAT the recommendation of the report titled “2025 Citizen of the Year – Kushiro
Cup Award”, dated March 10, 2026, be released at the March 24, 2026, Open
Council meeting; and
THAT staff be authorized to contact the recipients and nominators prior to
Council’s Open meeting.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
